title: I got it I GOT IT ALL
display_title: I got it I GOT IT ALL
wikitext:
[]: I got it! I GOT IT ALL!             Quote Post by phas » Wed Mar 12, 2025 12:14 pm I watched EVANGELION for the first time around 25 years ago. Then rewatched it, including the movies a couple of times. Like everyone else I understood nothing about it and, since I was a boy at the time, I became quite intrigued about it and started to speculate and make theories. I bet people in this forum can relate. Eventually I concluded that there was no explanation and stuff was just weird. So naturally, when decades later I first read about Classified Informations, I was quite shocked to learn that, actually, it all made some sense and may very well be planned from the beginning. To me CI made sense as an overall explanation of the facts in NGE. I also noticed that a lot of details were still missing. Like, what exactly were the different instrumentality scenarios. Why did someone put Lilith's soul into Rei? Why Kawuru was sent to NERV, etc. Then, since I wasn’t a boy anymore and not really that much into Japanese stuff anyway, I stopped thinking about it. But then, around a week ago, I stumbled on a youtube video about evangelion and somehow got into thinking about it again. I have watched Rebuild of Evangelion and it’s clear that in those movies, the CI background doesn’t apply very well. Now, if the CI is accurate to the original series, which I think it is, it means that those movies deviated from it. Ok, nothing new. But then, I had a thought. What if the same applies to End of Evangelion? I mean, what if the original NGE series was developed according to what transpires from the CIs, but then Anno messed with that in EoE? Then I started to think about NGE in light of CIs, without taking in consideration anything about EoE. That’s not typically what we do, because we tend to think of EoE as a retelling of the same events in episode 25/26 from a different perspective. Except it is not really. A lot more stuff happens in EoE. A lot of confusing stuff. Like the whole third impact / instrumentality in EoE is strange. It’s triggered by Shinji getting into 01 and going berserk AND Gendo doing stuff with Lilith and Rei and Adam… and then all is decided by Shinji’s will… It’s difficult to reconcile what we see with the Seele master plan. And all that was included in the dead sea scroll? The production series EVAs? How? Why? It’s complicated, it’s convoluted, it doesn’t make a lot of sense. That said, the first thing that came to me when I started to think this way, is that in NGE, Nerv doesn’t really seem to have any access to some advanced alien tech. What they have, they get by crudely interacting with FAR stuff they found. LCL? Poke a couple of holes into Lilith and drain the liquid. EVAs? Rip pieces of Lilith apart and let them regenerate. EVA controls? Drill a hole in the Core, stick a human inside. Repair EVAs? Let them regenerate and replace the armor plates. They have some sensors, they can detect AT fields and angels “waveforms” but apart from everything seem to be explained with simple interactions with FAR beings. Apart from soul harvesting. This is the only real “scifi” thing they seem to be able to do. Or are they? We will go back to that. Then I thought about EVAS. We tend to think about 01 as the only Lilith based EVA and therefore central for instrumentality. But is it? I mean, why? EVA 01 seems to be obtained by ripping parts from Lilith. It works, why bother trying to clone Adam. Even if they could, which seems suss, and even if they had a sample (remember that Adam embryo was recovered well after 00 was completed) what’s the benefit in doing that? I think all EVAs are simply obtained by ripping pieces from Lilith. That would explain why Lilith is still “incomplete” when they recover the Spear of Longinus, years after 01 is completed, because they tore pieces for Evas 03-04. I think that there’s nothing special about 01, other than the fact that it contains Yui’s soul and that makes it special only for Gendo. UNTIL it acquires an S2 engine, only THEN Seele starts to take a special interest in it. Mostly they are preoccupied by its existence, they don’t like the fact that Gendo has a semi godlike being at his disposal. Which makes me think that 01 nor any other EVA has any part in instrumentality. So, Instrumentality. I started thinking about that. I’m Italian, and in the Italian dub of NGE, “instrumentality project” is translated somewhat like “Project for human improvement”. I started to dig and I found that this is closer to the Japanese original. So what is “the improvement of humans” in the light of CIs? It seems pretty clear to me that the only possible meaning is to get the fruit of life to the human race. I mean, what else makes sense? The sea of LCL, the merging of all humans, is EoE stuff. But still I was getting nowhere. So I got back to the details. The soul stuff. How do they move souls around? Do they have a complex tech that is not shown? Maybe not, I mean Ritsuko says that “the Door of Guf is empty”, so the souls are around. When people die, they leave the body. When people are born, they must incarnate. Maybe they just get into the closer empty vessel. It seems to work like that when people die in an EVA. Except for Rei, that seems more complex… but maybe not. We know that souls can temporarily leave a body and they tend to return to it. Maybe it’s simply the same with a cloned body. They tend to return to the same body if available or move to the closer empty one. So there’s no “soul salvaging tech”, it’s just “place an empty vessel close to a dying person”. And then it hit me, like a ton of bricks. Kaworu died next to Lilith’s body. His soul must have incarnated into Lilith. Adam soul into Lilith. That’s the thing they were doing. They wanted to put Adam soul in Lilith, and Lilith soul into Adam. Then merge them. That’s instrumentality. Their contact experiment with Adam was to extract Adam soul and revert it into embryo. They knew it was going to explode, well someone knew. I mean if the dead sea scrolls are a emergency manual on how to do if a seed of life lands on your seed of knowledge civilization, it makes sense to say things “Watch out, if the seed of life has already started to reset all life, you can use the Spear of Longinus to stop it, but it might explode”. They must have figured out a way to shield rooms, spaces from souls so no random roaming soul can enter a vessel, like Rei or a new EVA. Then they extracted Lilith's soul (I don’t want to know how, probably they tortured it until the soul fled, or “killed” it somehow) and let it enter Rei by conveniently placing a cloned Rei near it. So they could swap them. That’s why Ritsuko killed Rei’s clones. Yeah she said she hated them. But these people lie. Lie, hide their motives, and plan in advance. With Rei clones gone, you just need Rei, Adam’s embryo, a gun and B(L)AM, you have Lilith's soul in Adam's body. That’s Ritsuko betraying Gendo. Because Gendo is trying to stop all this (“Death solves nothing”) and just reunite with Yui in EVA01. At that point instrumentality can be as simple as throwing Adam’s embryo in Lilith’s body, let them absorb it and you have created a complete Seed of Life with both the Fruit of Knowledge and the Fruit of Life. When it awakes, it will recognize that humans are not his “thing” and reset all life. All souls, both of humans and angels, will go back to the unified Door of Guf. That’s the third impact, as seen is ep 25/26. That’s why in these episodes, Shinji appears to be communicating with not only other humans but also angels souls! They are all togheter in the new seed of life's door of guf! That's why we cut from episode 24 with Shinji killing Kaworu and going home directly to instrumentality. Because the show is from Shinji perspective and he did not start instrumentality. Someone else did. Someone with a gun or guns, because we can see Misato got killed, probably trying to stop it. My money would be on Ritsuko, as a defiance to Gendo and in obedience to Seele, as the last dramatic plot twist that we are not even shown. Brilliant. Finally the new Seed of Life, will start to create new FAR-like complete beings, with both seed of life and knowledge, and the souls will start to incarnate in them. That’s the end of episode 26, with Shinji soul realizing that he can live as an individual. That’s the completion of human instrumentality, not the rejection of it, which makes much more sense. So yeah, that was long. But it all makes sense now! Can I swear of this forum? IT ALL F MAKES SENSE NOW!

[]: Re: I got it! I GOT IT ALL!             Quote Post by phas » Wed Mar 12, 2025 3:14 pm I want to explain why I think EoE doesn't make sense with CIs and why I think that it's a bit more that just my personal opinion. We have two possibilities. Either the third impact scenario is a direct implementation of what's written in the dead sea scrolls. In this case at some point they read something like that: "If you want to merge all humanity togheter tie Lilith to a cross. Revert Adam to a embryo. Place Adam embryo in someone arm, put the arm inside a clone of is wife. Put the clone of the wife in Lilith. Then put the severely emotionally damaged son of the guy who just gave the arm in a clone of Lilith with a S2 engine that you stole somewhere. Then get him mad about life. Then summon the 9 clones of other angels you have prepared in advance with their clone of spear of longinus. Chaos ensues. Let it stir for some time with fireworks and then the emotionally damaged child will decide if life will merge or not". I don't know about you. But if I found out my creator left that kind of note (and engineered things... me) with that kind of process I would immediately start a "human destrumentality project" aimed at distancing myself from them as fast as possible. The alternative is that mess is the result of humans engineering the third impact indirectly based on the knowledge that they gained from the dead sea scrolls. But that would mean that the dead sea scrolls contain a compendium of FAR science. Angels biology, lilin biology, seed of life biology, metaphysical biology, the whole thing. And that's not in NGE, it's simply not there! They struggle to control an EVA, a thing that supposedly they built. If they understood the whole thing, they just have built a "spear of longinus cannon" and obliterate the angels that way. These people don't have the knowledge. They are rebuilding life as we know it by drilling, hammering and shooting at stuff! And that's metal as hell! By looking at NGE alone and applying my reasoning, we can actually infer most of the contents of the dead sea scrolls. They must read something like that. "We are the FAR, we possess the fruit of knowledge and the fruit of life, we are dying. We sent seeds of life in the galaxy to continue life. However since [insert reason here] we don't want them to have both fruits, se every seed will have only one. Seeds of life contains our souls sealed inside the door of guf. When a seed of life will reach a planet, it will open the door of guf and disperse souls. Then will create life. Souls will inhabit living bodies. Souls will give shape to life forms using their at fields. Life with fruit of life will be immortal and powerful and big and have strange shapes with strong at fields. Life with fruit of knowledge will develop technology. Seeds of life travel with a Spear, that's a device that will inhibit a seed of life in case two seeds of life with both fruits find themselves on the same planet (which we really don't like). In this case one spear will deactivate one seed, all souls from that seed will re-enter its door of guf. If a seed of life enters in contact with a life being with a different fruit, it will create a powerful anti-at-field resetting all life on the planet and then proceed to re-create life. WARNING if for some reason a seed of life is in the process of resetting all life and is stopped by the Spear, it might explode, dying and releasing its soul, which might inhabit another vessel. We tried to fix this bug but we already were over time and over budget and by the way it shouldn't happen, right?" Quite simple, right? You can derive all events of NGE from Seele reading this, finding Adam, doing 2+2 and thinking "wait a minute, what if we merge Lilith and Adam togheter?". It fits and it's simple. Remember that Anno said that all needed to understand NGE was in NGE, so it SHOULD be simple.

[]: Re: I got it! I GOT IT ALL!             Quote Post by phas » Fri Sep 26, 2025 4:06 pm Up. Was this theory so bad that didn't even merit a response from anyone? I lost sleep on that, and re reading it after a while, still makes sense to me.

[]: Re: I got it! I GOT IT ALL!             Quote Post by Diego the Lego » Fri Sep 26, 2025 10:22 pm Well I am lost on what you are referring to by "Classified Informations" and what your theory is trying to say really. I don't believe that EoE is a retelling of episodes 25-26 nor do believe that are not fundamentally similar many ways. I think that the more apt way to put it is that EoE is a remake of episodes 25-26 that exist within the same continuity. If I'm understanding your theory correctly, you believe that EoE is entirely separate from the original ending, and that the original ending sees the completion of Instrumentality. I think that is the key point where your interpretations and mine differ as I view both endings having Shinji reject instrumentality because as you said Shinji realizes that he can live as an individual, in contrast to instrumentality where the very concept of an individual wouldn't exist at all. I believe that in both endings Shinji is the key instigator for instrumentality as it's his decision to make and forces him to confront his isolation and inability to connect with other people which is his main conflict throughout the series. The ways two endings go about showing this are wildly different though as in episodes 25-26 it is manifested on a far more conceptual level. Feel free to correct me if I've misinterpreted anything you said, but if you could elaborate more or on what exactly you're getting at and/or simplify your core points a bit more I would appreciate it since I am actually quite intrigued by what your perspective is exactly.

[]: Re: I got it! I GOT IT ALL!             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Oct 08, 2025 5:24 pm phas wrote: Up. Was this theory so bad that didn't even merit a response from anyone?
